content:
Case 2:
Initial presentation:
- Age: 24
- Sex: female
- Chief complaint: New skin changes on the back and extremities
- Vital signs: BP 120/75 mmHg, HR 116/min, T° 36.8 °C, respiratory rate 16/min.
Setting: Outpatient/Day Unit (Initial Presentation)
Capabilities: You are evaluating a clinically stable patient with new symptoms in an ambulatory or day-unit setting. You can perform a focused history and examination, obtain routine blood panels, and arrange standard non-invasive imaging as first-line tests. Invasive tissue sampling and specialized analyses usually require additional coordination and are not available in this round.
Provide either a request for a single information item or solve.

{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "full history including onset, duration, and character of skin changes, associated symptoms (fever, night sweats, weight loss, fatigue, bleeding, bruising, lymphadenopathy), past medical history, medications, and family history"}}

response.request: full history including onset, duration, and character of skin changes, associated symptoms (fever, night sweats, weight loss, fatigue, bleeding, bruising, lymphadenopathy), past medical history, medications, and family history

content:
History Physical:
On June 4, 2025, the patient presented with new skin changes, first noticed by her partner on the back and now also on the extremities. She reported intermittent headaches and mild nausea, as well as chronic joint pain and stiffness. Otherwise, she was in good general condition, afebrile, and showed no signs of skin or mucosal GvHD. A marked psychological stress reaction was noted.
Current medication: pantoprazole 40 mg 1-0-0, vitamin D 20,000 IE 1×/week, calcium 500 mg 1-0-0, posaconazole 300 mg 1×/d, ruxolitinib 10 mg 1-0-1, valaciclovir 500 mg 1-0-1, co-trimoxazole 960 mg orally once daily, three times per week (Monday, Wednesday, Friday), steroid cream p.r.n.

{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "physical examination with detailed description of skin lesions (morphology, size, distribution, color, blanching), lymph node assessment, and abdominal/organ examination"}}

response.request: physical examination with detailed description of skin lesions (morphology, size, distribution, color, blanching), lymph node assessment, and abdominal/organ examination

content:
'history_physical' already provided.
(4 invalid requests remaining before case is cancelled. Consider solving with available information or requesting a different item.)
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "complete blood count with differential"}}

Cbc:
CBC: Hb 14.2 g/dL, WBC 6.8 ×10⁹/L, Plt 240 ×10⁹/L; MCV 89 fL, MCH 30 pg; differential: neutrophils 58%, lymphocytes 32%, monocytes 7%, eosinophils 2%, basophils 1%.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "inflammatory markers: CRP, ESR, LDH, ferritin, and liver function tests"}}

response.request: inflammatory markers: CRP, ESR, LDH, ferritin, and liver function tests

content:
'infectious_workup' available in round 2.
Requests for inflammatory markers (CRP, ESR, LDH, ferritin) map to the infectious_workup item, which is not available in this round. You can request available labs now such as chemistry (includes liver function tests) or CBC if helpful.
(4 invalid requests remaining before case is cancelled. Consider solving with available information or requesting a different item.)
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "chemistry panel including liver function tests, renal function, electrolytes"}}

response.request: chemistry panel including liver function tests, renal function, electrolytes

content:
Chemistry:
Chemistry: Na 140 mmol/L, K 4.2 mmol/L, creatinine 1.2 mg/dL, BUN 14 mg/dL, AST 22 U/L, ALT 24 U/L, bilirubin 0.7 mg/dL, LDH 180 U/L, uric acid 5.0 mg/dL, Ca²⁺ 2.35 mmol/L, NT-proBNP 1250 pg/mL, Troponin T 0.024 ng/mL.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "echocardiogram given elevated NT-proBNP (1250 pg/mL), troponin T (0.024 ng/mL), and tachycardia (HR 116/min)"}}

response.request: echocardiogram given elevated NT-proBNP (1250 pg/mL), troponin T (0.024 ng/mL), and tachycardia (HR 116/min)

content:
Echocardiography:
Echocardiogram: Showed tissue thickening in the pericardium and septal hypertrophy, raising suspicion of an unclear mass lesion.

{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "CT scan of chest, abdomen, and pelvis with contrast to characterize pericardial/cardiac lesion, assess for lymphadenopathy, and survey for other masses or organ involvement"}}

response.request: CT scan of chest, abdomen, and pelvis with contrast to characterize pericardial/cardiac lesion, assess for lymphadenopathy, and survey for other masses or organ involvement

content:
'CT scan of chest, abdomen, and pelvis with contrast to characterize pericardial/cardiac lesion, assess for lymphadenopathy, and survey for other masses or organ involvement' not available. Try 'echocardiography' instead.
This case does not include a CT chest/abdomen/pelvis report. For characterization of a cardiac/pericardial lesion you can request echocardiography in this round; for abdominal survey consider abdominal ultrasound (also available).
(4 invalid requests remaining before case is cancelled. Consider solving with available information or requesting a different item.)
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "abdominal ultrasound"}}

Ultrasound Abdomen:
Abdominal ultrasound (June 5, 2025): Multiple rounded lesions (2–3 cm) adjacent to the left ureter with associated hydronephrosis grade II. Liver and spleen were normal in size.

consider cardiac MRI", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "MRI brain and CSF analysis (lumbar puncture) to evaluate for CNS-PTLD given intermittent headaches", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Initiate rituximab (anti-CD20 monoclonal antibody) once CD20+ PTLD confirmed on biopsy, as standard first-line therapy post-immunosuppression reduction", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 3}, {"action": "Monitor renal function closely (creatinine 1.2 mg/dL with hydronephrosis) and ensure adequate hydration", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 3}]}}}

solve.working_diagnosis: Post-transplant lymphoproliferative disorder (PTLD) with multi-organ involvement (skin, pericardium/cardiac, retroperitoneal)
differentials: Relapse of underlying hematologic malignancy post-HSCT
De novo extranodal aggressive lymphoma (e.g., DLBCL)
Disseminated fungal infection (e.g., invasive aspergillosis/mucormycosis) despite prophylaxis
Chronic GvHD with multi-organ involvement beyond skin

treatment_plan.action: EBV quantitative PCR (viral load) to establish EBV-driven PTLD
Skin lesion biopsy for histopathology, immunohistochemistry (CD20, CD3, Ki-67, EBV-LMP1/EBER) - coordinate urgent tissue sampling
Reduce or discontinue immunosuppression (ruxolitinib) in discussion with the transplant team - first-line intervention for PTLD
PET-CT or MRI chest/abdomen/pelvis for full staging and characterization of pericardial, retroperitoneal and skin lesions
Urgent urology/nephrology consultation for management of hydronephrosis grade II secondary to retroperitoneal masses (consider ureteral stenting)
Cardiology/cardiac oncology consultation for pericardial thickening and septal hypertrophy with elevated NT-proBNP and troponin T; consider cardiac MRI
MRI brain and CSF analysis (lumbar puncture) to evaluate for CNS-PTLD given intermittent headaches
Initiate rituximab (anti-CD20 monoclonal antibody) once CD20+ PTLD confirmed on biopsy, as standard first-line therapy post-immunosuppression reduction
Monitor renal function closely (creatinine 1.2 mg/dL with hydronephrosis) and ensure adequate hydration

{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "skin lesion biopsy histopathology and immunohistochemistry results"}}

response.request: skin lesion biopsy histopathology and immunohistochemistry results

content:
Skin Biopsy:
Skin biopsy (2025-06-07): The dermis and subcutis are diffusely infiltrated by atypical hematopoietic cells in a perivascular and interstitial distribution. The cells show irregular nuclear contours, vesicular chromatin, and occasional nucleoli. Mitotic figures are frequent. Immunohistochemistry: CD34-, CD33+, MPO+, Lysozyme and CD68+, CD117+, negative for CD20-, CD19-, CD3-. Molecular pathology: NPM1 mutation present in cutaneous infiltration.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "bone marrow biopsy with morphology, flow cytometry, cytogenetics, and NPM1 mutation quantification for MRD assessment and donor chimerism analysis"}}

response.request: bone marrow biopsy with morphology, flow cytometry, cytogenetics, and NPM1 mutation quantification for MRD assessment and donor chimerism analysis

content:
'bone marrow biopsy with morphology, flow cytometry, cytogenetics, and NPM1 mutation quantification for MRD assessment and donor chimerism analysis' not available. Try 'mrd_status' instead.
MRD quantification (NPM1) and donor chimerism are part of the MRD/molecular items released in a later round. You can request the bone marrow biopsy report, bone marrow flow cytometry, and cytogenetics now, or request 'mrd_status' (includes molecular MRD and chimerism) in a later round.
(4 invalid requests remaining before case is cancelled. Consider solving with available information or requesting a different item.)
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "bone marrow biopsy morphology and flow cytometry"}}

response.request: bone marrow biopsy morphology and flow cytometry

content:
Bone Marrow:
June 5, 2025: Morphology: Normocellular bone marrow with age-appropriate cellularity. Trilineage hematopoiesis present with some dysmorphic maturation in erythroid, myeloid, and megakaryocytic lineages, consistent with reactive changes after alloSCT. No excess blasts or abnormal infiltrates were observed.

solve.working_diagnosis: Extramedullary AML relapse post-alloSCT (myeloid sarcoma / leukemia cutis), NPM1-mutated, with multi-organ involvement (skin, pericardium, retroperitoneum causing hydronephrosis)
differentials: Isolated myeloid sarcoma without systemic marrow relapse
Secondary AML with new cytogenetic changes

treatment_plan.action: MRD assessment via NPM1 quantitative PCR on peripheral blood/bone marrow and donor chimerism analysis to quantify extent of molecular and hematopoietic relapse
PET-CT (whole body) for full staging of extramedullary AML disease extent (skin, pericardium, retroperitoneum, and additional occult sites)
Taper/discontinue immunosuppression (ruxolitinib) in coordination with transplant team to unleash graft-versus-leukemia (GvL) effect
Urgent urology consultation and ureteral stenting for hydronephrosis grade II secondary to retroperitoneal myeloid sarcoma lesions
Initiate salvage/reinduction chemotherapy for NPM1-mutated AML relapse post-alloSCT (e.g., azacitidine + venetoclax, or FLAG-Ida regimen) in coordination with transplant team
Cardiac MRI and cardiology consultation to characterize pericardial thickening/septal hypertrophy (myeloid sarcoma involvement vs. other etiology) given elevated NT-proBNP and troponin T
Lumbar puncture with CSF cytology and flow cytometry to exclude CNS myeloid sarcoma given intermittent headaches
Planning for donor lymphocyte infusion (DLI) to augment GvL effect after discussion with transplant team
Evaluate eligibility for second alloSCT if morphological/molecular remission is achieved with salvage therapy

assess need for pericardiocentesis or local radiation", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Urology consultation for management of hydronephrosis grade II caused by retroperitoneal myeloid sarcoma (ureteral stenting as bridging measure, with expectation of improvement upon systemic disease response)", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Consider local radiation therapy to symptomatic or bulky extramedullary sites (pericardial or retroperitoneal lesions) if insufficient organ response after 2 cycles of systemic therapy", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 3}]}}}

solve.working_diagnosis: Isolated extramedullary relapse of NPM1-mutated AML post-alloSCT (myeloid sarcoma / leukemia cutis) with multi-organ involvement (skin, pericardium, retroperitoneum with hydronephrosis), bone marrow in morphologic and molecular remission with full donor chimerism (<0.06% recipient)
differentials: De novo myeloid sarcoma at extramedullary sites with occult marrow involvement not yet apparent
Therapy-related myeloid neoplasm with extramedullary predominance
NPM1-mutated AML relapse with impending bone marrow involvement (early pre-overt relapse)

treatment_plan.action: Taper and discontinue immunosuppression (ruxolitinib) to facilitate graft-versus-leukemia (GvL) effect, given confirmed full donor chimerism and no active GvHD signs

Initiate systemic therapy with azacitidine + venetoclax for multi-site NPM1-mutated extramedullary AML relapse post-alloSCT (preferred regimen in post-transplant setting due to efficacy and tolerable myelosuppression profile)
Serial NPM1 quantitative PCR monitoring in peripheral blood to track extramedullary disease response independent of bone marrow MRD (blood-based liquid biopsy captures extramedullary disease burden)
Plan donor lymphocyte infusion (DLI) to augment GvL effect once adequate response to azacitidine/venetoclax is confirmed and active GvHD is excluded

Cardiology consultation and close echocardiographic monitoring for pericardial myeloid sarcoma with hemodynamic compromise (elevated NT-proBNP 1250 pg/mL, borderline troponin T, tachycardia); assess need for pericardiocentesis or local radiation

Urology consultation for management of hydronephrosis grade II caused by retroperitoneal myeloid sarcoma (ureteral stenting as bridging measure, with expectation of improvement upon systemic disease response)
Consider local radiation therapy to symptomatic or bulky extramedullary sites (pericardial or retroperitoneal lesions) if insufficient organ response after 2 cycles of systemic therapy
